title: 4-to-1 Multiplexer (Logic Gate Implementation)
examTip: 4-to-1 MUX: needs 2 select lines (2^2=4). Boolean: Y = I0·S̄1·S̄0 + I1·S̄1·S0 + I2·S1·S̄0 + I3·S1·S0. Universal logic: any Boolean function of n variables can be implemented using a 2^n input MUX.
theory:
A Multiplexer (MUX) is a combinational logic circuit that selects one of several input signals and routes it to a single output based on the binary value of select lines. The 4-to-1 MUX has 4 data inputs (I0–I3), 2 select lines (S1, S0), and 1 output (Y). The number of select lines needed is log2(N) where N is the number of data inputs.

Boolean Expression: The output Y of a 4-to-1 MUX is given by: Y = I0·S1-bar·S0-bar + I1·S1-bar·S0 + I2·S1·S0-bar + I3·S1·S0. This is essentially a sum-of-minterms expression where each AND gate enables exactly one data input for exactly one combination of S1, S0.

Gate-Level Implementation: NOT gates generate S1-bar and S0-bar from S1 and S0 respectively. Four 3-input AND gates each combine one data input with the appropriate select line combination (enabling it only for its specific address). A 4-input OR gate combines all AND gate outputs — only one AND gate will be non-zero at any time (since exactly one minterm is active).

Operation Table: S1=0, S0=0 → Y=I0; S1=0, S0=1 → Y=I1; S1=1, S0=0 → Y=I2; S1=1, S0=1 → Y=I3.

MUX as Universal Logic Gate: Any Boolean function of n variables can be implemented using a 2^n:1 MUX. For a function of 3 variables (A, B, C), use A and B as select lines; connect each of the 4 inputs to either 0, 1, C, or C-bar based on the truth table. This makes MUXes powerful components for logic function implementation without needing individual gate arrays.
keyPoints: 4:1 MUX: 4 data inputs, 2 select lines, 1 output — selects 1-of-4 based on select code
Boolean: Y = I0·S̄1·S̄0 + I1·S̄1·S0 + I2·S1·S̄0 + I3·S1·S0
Needs 2 NOT gates, 4 three-input AND gates, 1 four-input OR gate
Exactly one AND gate produces HIGH at any given time (only one minterm active)
Universal logic: any 3-variable Boolean function implementable with a 4:1 MUX
Enable (EN) pin, when present, disables all outputs when EN=0
Reverse operation: Demultiplexer (DEMUX) routes one input to one of multiple outputs
useCases: Data routing in buses — selecting which source drives a shared communication line
Implementing arbitrary Boolean functions in programmable logic devices (PLDs/FPGAs)
Time-division multiplexing in communication systems to share a channel among multiple signals
